sections.a_eyebrow: Experimental · Broken Grid · Deconstructed · Heavy Motion · Creative
sections.a_description: The distinctive visual language of Anti-Gravity Layout — combining experimental, broken grid, deconstructed elements to create interfaces that are immediately recognizable and purposefully designed. Every color, font choice, and spacing decision works together to evoke the specific mood and cultural context that defines this aesthetic, from the primary accent color to the carefully chosen typography.

This style finds its natural home across websites and applications that value experimental and broken grid design principles. You'll see it on creative portfolios, brand microsites, specialized platforms, and digital experiences where the visual language itself communicates as powerfully as the content — proving that the right aesthetic choice can transform a standard layout into a memorable experience.

a_image_cover.alt: Anti-Gravity Layout style use case illustration
Anti-Gravity Layout style use case illustration
Anti-Gravity Layout style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The distinctive visual aesthetic of Anti-Gravity Layout — a design approach that creates unique, memorable interfaces. Background: light (#fafaff). The .inner panel has a clean background with subtle border treatment and appropriate border-radius. Colors: #6366f1 as the primary accent for interactive elements and highlights. #1e1b4b for headings, #64748b for body text. The aesthetic captures the essence of anti-gravity layout through careful color choices, spacing, and visual weight. Set in Outfit — chosen for its visual character that complements this design style. Heading at weight 600-700, 24-28px, line-height 1.2-1.3. Eyebrow at weight 500, 11px, uppercase, 2-3px letter-spacing, color #6366f1. Body at weight 400, 14-15px, line-height 1.6-1.7, color #64748b. Buttons: primary — #6366f1 background with contrasting text. Secondary: transparent with #6366f1 border. Alternatives: system-ui (universal fallback), sans-serif stack.
